title: Limits of Recurrent Seq2Seq
concept: Even with attention, recurrent encoders cannot process tokens in parallel, making training on huge web corpora too slow. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Transformer Foundations.'

exercises.prompt: How do recurrent neural networks (RNNs) process a sequence of words?

options.text: All at the exact same time (parallel)

exercises.explain: Recurrent neural networks process tokens one after another, which makes them sequential.

cards.title: The Need for Speed
cards.body: We added attention to help Seq2Seq models remember long sentences, but they still have a major flaw: they are incredibly slow! Let's explore why recurrent models struggle to scale up to the massive datasets we use today.

cards.title: The Recurrent Bottleneck
cards.body: Recurrent Seq2Seq models process text step-by-step. Even with attention helping them remember details, they must wait for the previous word to finish processing before starting the next. This creates a massive speed bottleneck.

cards.title: The One-Word-at-a-Time Reader
cards.body: Imagine trying to read a massive book where you are only allowed to read one word at a time, and you must completely finish understanding a sentence before moving to the next. You can't skim the whole page at once! That is how a recurrent model reads text—word by painful word.

cards.title: Step-by-Step Processing
cards.body: If you feed the sentence 'The quick brown fox jumps' into a recurrent model, it must calculate the representation for 'The', then 'quick', then 'brown', and so on. It cannot calculate the representation for 'fox' until the previous three are completely finished.

cards.title: Myth: Attention Fixed Everything
cards.body: Many people think attention completely solved the speed issues of Seq2Seq models. In reality, while attention improved memory, it did not change the step-by-step nature of recurrence. The model still processes text chronologically, making training on huge web corpora too slow.

cards.title: Sequential vs. Parallel
cards.body: Let's compare how recurrent models process data versus what we actually want for fast training.

right.text: Processes all words at once. Fast and parallel.

exercises.prompt: What is the main limitation of Recurrent Seq2Seq models when training on massive datasets?

options.text: They cannot process tokens in parallel, making training too slow.
They do not support attention mechanisms.
They can only translate between English and French.
They require too much manual programming of grammar rules.

exercises.explain: The major bottleneck is that recurrence prevents us from calculating the representations of all words in parallel, making training on large datasets extremely slow.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Adding attention to a recurrent model allows it to process all input tokens at the same time in parallel.
exercises.explain: Even with attention, recurrent models still process tokens one-by-one in sequence, meaning they cannot process them in parallel.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe why recurrent models are slow.
exercises.sentence: Because they process words one by one, recurrent models suffer from a sequential bottleneck.
exercises.explain: Recurrent neural networks are forced to be sequential because each step relies on the calculations from the previous step.

exercises.prompt: Identify the realistic engineer's complaint about recurrent seq2seq models.

options.text: Our training runs are taking weeks because our recurrent encoder has to wait for each word to finish before starting the next. We need a parallel approach.
We speed up our recurrent encoder by running it backwards and forwards at the same time to achieve 100% parallel processing of the entire internet.
Recurrent networks are slow because they do not use computers to calculate math, relying instead on manual calculations.

exercises.explain: Only the first option represents a human writer's observation about training speeds. The other two show AI-like confusion about how recurrent models work.

exercises.prompt: Why can't recurrent Seq2Seq models process a whole paragraph of text at the exact same time during training?

options.text: They process all tokens simultaneously using parallel GPU threads
Each step relies on the previous step's output, forcing sequential processing
They do not support attention mechanisms at all
They only work on text that has fewer than ten words

exercises.explain: Exactly! Recurrent seq2seq models are stuck processing sequences step-by-step. Because of this, they cannot take advantage of modern GPUs to train on massive datasets in parallel. Next up: Transformer Foundations.
